title: Assembly of Endometrial Scaffold Systems
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d2sfu","text":"This protocol is a part of our collection of protocols for culturing human embryos and human stem cell-derived blastoids in an endometrial culture system.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aps4b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eqra0","text":"This protocol follows the protocols for the culture of human endometrial stromal and epithelial cells and it covers the assembly of the scaffold system using the cells cultured in these previous steps.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ks9n","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2j8mr","text":"It is recommended that you familiarise yourself with the protocols for embryo or blastoid preparation and culture in endometrial scaffolds so that you can have one of these ready to implant into this system and know what preparation this system needs prior to implantation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare human endometrial epithelial cells and Organoid Medium according to this protocol: Culture of Human Endometrial Epithelial Cells by Richard J Acton
Culture endometrial stromal cells according to the following protocol in preparation for their use in assembling the endometrial model: Culture of Human Endometrial Stromal Cells by Richard J Acton

After the stromal cells exhibit elongated fibroblast-like morphology, the next step adds endometrial epithelial cells on top of the stromal compartment. One near-confluent dome of endometrial organoids provides a sufficient number of cells to seed two transwells. Disaggregate the endometrial epithelial organoids as described previously (refer to our "Culture of Human Endometrial Epithelial Cells" protocol), aiming to keep the fragments reasonably large and avoid very small pieces or single cells.

To assemble the endometrial model, first prepare the extracellular matrix (ECM) protein stock solutions: Collagen Type III : Human Collagen Type III Rockland Catalog # 009-001-105 Supplied at: 1 mg/mL in 0.5M acetic acid, pH 2.5. Store at -20 °C or 4 °C . Collagen Type I : Human Collagen Type I Rockland Catalog #009-001-103 Supplied at: 1 mg/mL in 0.5M acetic acid, pH 2.5. Store at -20 °C or 4 °C Collagen Type VI : Native Human Collagen VI protein Abcam Catalog #ab7538 Supplied at: 1.13 mg/mL 0.01% sodium azide, 3% acetic acid. Store at 4 °C . Fibronectin : Human Plasma Fibronectin Purified Protein Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #FC010 Supplied at: 1 mg/mL in 150mM sodium chloride, 10mM sodium phosphate, pH 7.5 Store at 4 °C . Hydrogel ECM Protein Mix : %s %s

To make 30μl of dextran–ECM Hydrogel solution : In a low protein-binding PCR tube , mix 2.5 µL TrueGel3D (TRU-B72) buffer from Hydrogel Kit (vortex stock solution well before use to dissolve any precipitates) with 2 µL SLO-DEXTRAN solution (TRU-SGD) from Hydrogel Kit , and 6 µL of Hydrogel ECM Protein Mix (prepared as above). The solution will turn yellow because of its low pH. Incubate the mix On ice for at least 1 hour 01:00:00 . Hydrogel Kit : TrueGel3D Hydrogel Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #TRUE7-1KT true7bul.pdf

Neutralise the pH of the dextran–ECM Hydrogel solution by adding: 8.75 µL Stromal Medium (10% FBS) (defined in "Culture of Human Endometrial Stromal Cells") 0.75 µL of 1M HEPES pH 7.5 HEPES 1M Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #15630080 to give final concentration of 25 millimolar (mM) 2.3 µL of 1 Molarity (M) sodium bicarbonate (Dissolve Sodium bicarbonate P212121 in PBS DPBS with MgCl2 and CaCl2 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #D8662 for 1M concentration, and sterile filter ) Incubate the solution at Room temperature for 01:00:00 . Check the colour of the solution, it should change from yellow to orange/red, indicating neutral pH. Neutralising the solution often takes several rounds of adding extra 1M sodium bicarbonate. We typically add ~0.2ul of 1M sodium bicarbonate to the Hydrogel-ECM Mixture and then wait for 00:30:00 before checking the indicator colour and deciding whether to add a further 0.2ul of 1M sodium bicarbonate. Repeat this gradual addition until the pH is fully neutralised.

Harvest endometrial stromal cells and mix them with the cell-degradable crosslinker (TRU-CD) from the Hydrogel Kit as follows: 100,000 stromal cells in 5 µL Stromal Medium (10% FBS) 3 µL cell-degradable crosslinker (TRU-CD) Mix well by pipetting. Add the 8 µL of this stromal cells-crosslinker mixture to the ~ 22 µL of Hydrogel-ECM Mixture . Mix well by pipetting to make Stromal Cell-Hydrogel Mixture . Aim to have the stromal cells at ~80% before they are harvested. Avoid letting the stromal cells grow to confluence, as this will affect their subsequent growth in the hydrogel.

Construction of the Cell-engineered Receptive Endometrial Scaffold Technology (CREST) is a multi-step process and begins with the assembly of a stromal compartment inside a transwell. Thincert Cell Culture Insert for 24 Well Plates (Cell Culture Well Insert, Greiner, 662630, 24 Well Thincert, 3.0µm pore diameter, transparent, sterile, https://shop.gbo.com/en/england/products/bioscience/cell-culture-products/thincert-cell-culture-inserts/thincert-cell-culture-inserts-6well/662630.html , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/ntm4bwv5f.jpg )

Transfer 30 µL of the Stromal Cell-Hydrogel Mixture to the centre of the transwell. Carefully invert the plate (typically a 24-well plate ) so that it is upside down and place the plate inside a tissue culture incubator for 00:02:00 at 37 °C This begins the gel polymerisation and helps to distribute the stromal cells evenly throughout the hydrogel. After 00:02:00 , remove plate from incubator and transfer plate to a tissue culture cabinet . Lift the plate and allow it to drop onto the surface inside the cabinet. Repeat twice. Ensure that the drop flattens and that the borders of the well and filled in without a meniscus. Incubate plate for a further 00:20:00 at 37 °C in the tissue culture incubator to allow the gel to polymerise.

Add 1 mL of pre-warmed Stromal Medium (10% FBS) into the lower compartment of the transwell and 250 µL of pre-warmed Stromal Medium (10% FBS) into the upper compartment. Incubate for 00:20:00 at 37 °C inside a tissue culture incubator . Remove the 250μL medium from the upper compartment and replace with fresh 250 µL of pre-warmed Stromal Medium (10% FBS) . This wash step helps to remove excess buffer and other components. Add sterile PBS DPBS with MgCl2 and CaCl2 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #D8662 to any empty wells of the 24-well plate to minimise evaporation of the media.
Incubate transwells at 37 °C , 20% atmospheric O 2 and 5% CO 2 and change media every 48:00:00 , until stromal cells reach full elongation (typically after 6-9 days).

To prepare Organoid Medium (2% FBS) , dilute (charcoal-stripped) CS FBS Fetal Bovine Serum (USA origin, Charcoal Stripped, sterile-filtered, suitable for cell culture) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #F6765 to 2% in Organoid Medium . Resuspend endometrial epithelial fragments in 300 µL Organoid Medium (2% FBS). Remove the stromal medium from the upper compartment of the transwell. Wash the upper compartment of the transwell with DMEM/F-12 and remove medium. Transfer 300 µL of the fragmented organoids to the upper compartment. Incubate cells at 37 °C with atmospheric O 2 and 5% CO 2 . After 48:00:00 , replace medium in upper compartment with fresh Organoid Medium (2% FBS) , taking care to avoid disturbing the loosely-attached fragments. Some outgrowth of the epithelial fragments should be visible at this stage.
Culture cells until the outgrowths from the epithelial fragments form a continuous epithelial monolayer covering the entire upper surface of the transwell (this typically takes ~5-7 days).
creator: Richard J Acton
